items.title: Adelaide employer to pay $25k penalty for not providing adequate meal breaks

children.content: An automotive industry group made threats "to create fear" in a man who had brought legal action against his employer for not giving him adequate lunch breaks, a tribunal judgement has found.

items.title: SA confirms second H5 bird flu case, two more suspected in Perth and WA

children.content: South Australia confirms its second H5 bird flu case while Western Australia investigates two more suspected detections, as authorities say the virus has not spread beyond isolated wild birds.

image.altText: Clare scriven speaks into media microphones while Kyam Maher stands next to a map showing SA

items.title: Court case against chair of Indian steelmaker won't affect Whyalla sale, SA government says

children.content: The chairperson of one of the bidders for the stricken Whyalla steelworks has been summonsed to appear before an Indian court, but the government says its confident the sale process will maintain integrity.

image.altText: A factory and furnace against the backdrop of sunset and a lake in the distance

items.title: Cost-of-living crisis 'hits from two directions' as major musicals close

children.content: As two more major musicals bite the dust, the local theatre industry hopes a live performance incentive modelled on a successful UK scheme will provide salvation.

image.altText: A man and a woman both dressed in blue sit beside each other on a park bench

items.title: Teen SA country footballer forced to find new sport after eight concussions

children.content: Angus Pitman finds a new sporting passion after walking away from country footy due to coping eight concussions on the field.

image.altText: a young man holds a red AFL football staring at camera

items.title: Hikers warned to take 'gnarly' peri-urban parks seriously after rescue

children.content: Three teenagers were rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park unscathed on Sunday, showing how quickly hikers can come unstuck on spectacular walking trails despite their proximity to Adelaide.

items.title: Algae which caused brevetoxins in SA algal bloom most toxic of its kind studied

children.content: The dominant species in the algal bloom which devastated SA's coastline,
Karenia cristata
, is the most toxic of its kind studied, according to newly published research.

image.altText: Dead marine life at Adelaide's West Beach.

items.title: Teens reported over 'fight club' as SA Police consider new laws

children.content: SA Police has reported three teenagers for allegedly fighting in a public place after amateur children's "fight club" videos were posted online, while the state's police commissioner says new laws could be introduced to make similar bouts harder to organise.

image.altText: SA Police Commissioner Grant Stevens is standing and wearing a full navy blue police uniform including police hat

items.title: 'Faeces, urine and dirt': Piggery owners in court on animal cruelty charges

children.content: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

image.altText: A man has short grey hair, he wears a dark jumper and walks outside a brick court building surrounded by reporters

items.title: Fishers buoyed as algal bloom-related whiting ban lifted early

children.content: A temporary ban on recreational catches of King George whiting along parts of the SA coast is lifted earlier than scheduled, in a move that fishers say will benefit the whole sector.

image.altText: Two King George whiting in hands of a man.

items.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

items.title: Farmer celebrates lamb birth boom as twins and triplets thrive

children.content: A wet start to 2026 in regional South Australia has helped create perfect conditions for multiple lamb births on one merino sheep stud.

image.altText: A man with a red hat is smiling holding two black and white speckled sheep.

items.title: New South Wales writes a new fate in blood after epic decider

children.content: Against all odds and with most of their state doubting them, Laurie Daley's New South Wales side has pulled off one of the greatest wins in Blues history.

articles.title: Quick hits: Arrow's standing 'O' and Queensland 'ripped off'

children.content: Jai Arrow is the only Queenslander to touch the State of Origin trophy as the Blues dominate the decider, but a NSW great questions a pivotal try. Here are your quick hits from the State of Origin decider.

image.altText: Reece Walsh, Jai Arrow and Nathan Cleary in State of Origin III 2026.

articles.title: The other edge cuts back on the Maroons in shock Origin loss

children.content: The Maroons under Billy Slater have gone all in on speed, attack and flair. On the biggest stage in the Origin decider, when things went against them, it all turned a bit too frantic.

image.altText: Queensland Maroons players watch Nathan Cleary line up another goal in State of Origin.

articles.title: Cleary 'silences the critics' as NSW make Origin history with decider win

children.content: NSW has produced a historic performance to win the State of Origin series with a 30-12 victory over Queensland at Lang Park in Brisbane.

items.title: The UK's next PM has a carefully curated image. Not everyone is buying it

items.title: Modi met with support and human rights protests as he arrives in Melbourne

items.title: As snowfall declines, a key ingredient is keeping the ski season alive

items.title: Melbourne injecting room cut ambulance callouts by 70pc, report reveals

children.content: Data from a new paper shows the number of heroin-related ambulance call-outs dropped from 48 a month to 14 a month in the first five years following the Richmond medically supervised injecting room's opening.

items.title: Aboriginal activist tells court of obligation to prevent 'slaughtered' forests

children.content: An Aboriginal activist has told a Tasmanian court she has a cultural obligation to "protect lands that are being slaughtered", after she was charged with trespassing while protesting in a logging coupe.

items.title: Woman at centre of university corruption claims fronts ICAC inquiry
